title: Support Worker
description:
We are recruiting Support Workers to assist vulnerable individuals across Leeds. You will support daily living activities, promote independence, and ensure a safe and supportive environment.

Duties include personal care, assisting with routines, and engaging clients in meaningful activities. You will work as part of a team delivering high-quality care services.

Previous experience in a care setting is desirable. Flexibility and a compassionate approach are essential.
summary: Support vulnerable individuals in Leeds with daily care and independence-focused support.

title: Care Assistant
description:
Care Assistants are required to support individuals with personal care and daily living tasks in a residential setting in Leeds.

Responsibilities include assisting with hygiene, mobility, and meal preparation while maintaining dignity and respect.

You will work closely with colleagues to deliver consistent, person-centred care. Training and development opportunities are available.
summary: Provide hands-on care support in a Leeds residential setting with a focus on dignity and wellbeing.

title: Healthcare Assistant
description:
Healthcare Assistants are needed to support patients in clinical and community environments across Leeds.

You will assist with patient care, monitor wellbeing, and support healthcare professionals in delivering services.

Strong communication skills and a caring attitude are essential. Previous experience is beneficial but not always required.
summary: Assist patients and clinical teams across Leeds in varied healthcare settings.

title: Residential Support Worker
description:
Join a residential care team supporting individuals with complex needs in Wakefield.

Responsibilities include providing emotional support, assisting with routines, and maintaining a structured environment.

You will work closely with colleagues to deliver consistent care and support outcomes.
summary: Deliver structured residential support for individuals with complex needs in Wakefield.

title: Support Worker
description:
Support Workers required in Bradford to assist individuals with daily activities and promote independence.

You will provide personal care, support mobility, and help individuals engage in daily routines.

The role requires a caring nature and commitment to high-quality support delivery.
summary: Provide day-to-day support helping individuals live independently in Bradford.

title: Care Assistant
description:
Care Assistants needed in Huddersfield to provide community-based support.

You will assist clients in their homes, supporting personal care, medication prompts, and daily routines.

Flexibility and reliability are essential for this role.
summary: Community-based care role supporting clients in their own homes in Huddersfield.

title: Support Worker
description:
Support Worker role assisting young people with behavioural and learning needs in Halifax.

You will provide structured support, encourage engagement, and assist with development activities.

Experience working with young people is beneficial.
summary: Support young people with behavioural needs in a structured Halifax setting.

title: Healthcare Assistant
description:
Healthcare Assistants required in Dewsbury to support patients with daily care needs.

Duties include monitoring wellbeing, assisting with routines, and supporting healthcare teams.

A caring and reliable approach is essential.
summary: Assist patients with care and daily routines in Dewsbury healthcare settings.

title: Care Assistant
description:
Care Assistants required in Batley to provide personal care and daily support.

You will assist individuals with routines, hygiene, and general wellbeing while ensuring dignity is maintained.

Teamwork and flexibility are key to success in this role.
summary: Provide essential personal care and daily support to individuals in Batley.

title: Support Worker
description:
Support Worker position in Castleford assisting individuals with varying needs.

You will help with daily routines, provide emotional support, and ensure a safe environment.

The role requires patience, empathy, and a commitment to high standards of care.
summary: Deliver person-centred support for individuals in a Castleford care setting.
